shower.thought: # Shower Thoughts
The people who brag about how busy they are are often the least productive—they're confusing motion with progress.
You'll never know if your childhood was happy or sad until you become an adult and realize you have nothing to compare it to.
We assume silence means nothing is happening, but a tree growing makes no sound.
The older you get, the more you realize that most of your anxiety was about things that required zero action.
You can't miss something you never had, which is why ignorance feels a lot like contentment.
Forgiveness isn't about making peace with someone else—it's about stopping them from living in your head rent-free.
The moment you stop trying to be interesting is usually when you become interesting.
We call it "falling" in love but it feels more like finally understanding gravity.
You've probably had the same thought as thousands of people today, but at a completely different time, making it feel original.
Social media made us realize that most people's lives are just them waiting for something worth posting about.
Nostalgia is just sadness with better marketing.
The hardest part of learning something isn't understanding it—it's unlearning everything wrong you thought you knew.
You can be right and still lose because being right doesn't pay your bills.
Most arguments aren't about the thing being argued—they're about who gets to decide what things mean.
A scar is just a story your body tells when you stop talking about it.
We spend our whole lives running from boredom, but boredom might just be your brain asking you to think.
The worst part of starting something isn't the difficulty—it's knowing that doing nothing was always an option.
Complaining about your problems is sometimes just you asking for permission to keep them.
You know you're getting old when you start feeling sorry for people instead of judging them.

motivational.thought: The master has failed more times than the beginner has even tried. — Stephen McCranie
Progress whispers while regret screams. Choose which one you'll listen to. — Unknown
Your comfort zone is not a life sentence. It's a waiting room. — Unknown
The only person you're destined to become is the one you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Regret for things we did can be tempered by time; it is regret for the things we did not do that is inconsolable. — Sydney Smith
Small daily progress compounds into extraordinary results. This is not philosophy—it's math. — Unknown
You are not a finished product. You are a work in progress, and that's exactly where your power lives. — Unknown
The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek. — Joseph Campbell
What you resist persists. What you act on transforms. — Unknown
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Augustus William Hare
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to quit. — Unknown
Your life is not a rehearsal. Stop waiting for the conditions to be perfect; they won't be. — Unknown
The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it. — Chinese Proverb
Bravery is not the absence of fear—it's action in spite of it. — Unknown
Success is the sum of small efforts repeated day in and day out. — Robert Collier
You can't change the wind, but you can adjust your sails. — Dolly Parton
The gap between your current self and your future self is just consistent decisions. — Unknown
Tomorrow is promised to no one. Make today count. — Unknown
The only failure is failing to try. Everything else is just data. — Unknown
Motivation is what gets you started. Habit is what keeps you going. — Jim Ryun

darkjoke.thought:
I told my girlfriend she was drawing her eyebrows too high.
She looked surprised.

My therapist asked what my biggest fear is.
I said, "Abandonment." She said we'd continue next week and never called back.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.

People keep asking if I'm okay after my breakup.
No, but I appreciate you checking in every five minutes on social media where my ex can see it.

My doctor told me I need to watch my drinking.
So now I drink in front of a mirror.

I said to my friend, "I'm worried nobody likes me."
He said, "Don't be ridiculous." Then he blocked me.

My parents said they'd always be there for me.
They were right—nowhere else would have them.

I asked the genie for the ability to predict the future.
He said, "You'll regret this." He was right, but I'm still furious he wouldn't explain why.

My wife asked if I was seeing someone else.
I said, "Only when you're not looking, and the guilt is eating me alive."
She said, "No, I mean—" "Oh. No."

I'm reading a book on anti-gravity.
Can't put it down. Tried to throw it away but it keeps coming back to me.

My friend said therapy changed his life.
I said, "How much does it cost?" He said, "Everything." I think he misunderstood the question.

I tried to tell a chemistry joke but there was no reaction.
My therapist says I need to accept failure better. We're not making much progress.

I have trust issues.
My therapist says it's because of my childhood. I believe him. I have no choice, he has my insurance information.

My boss asked why I looked so depressed at work.
I said, "Honestly? This place." He said, "Well, let's fix that," and now I'm unemployed.

I told my mother-in-law a secret and asked her to keep it confidential.
She said, "Of course." Then she told everyone at her book club, which is just code for the neighborhood Facebook group.

They say money can't buy happiness.
But it can buy a jet ski. Have you ever seen someone frown on a jet ski? You have, because that was me, and the jet ski was repossessed.

I went to a vegan restaurant and told them I didn't feel welcome.
They said, "Everyone's welcome here." I said, "Exactly. That's the problem."

My son asked me what happens when we die.
I said, "Nobody knows." He said, "But you always know everything." I've never felt so validated and so mortal.

I told my date I was an open book.
Turns out I'm more of a pamphlet. With typos. That they returned.

People say I'm too negative.
I think that's unfair. Sometimes I'm very positive—positively certain everything will go wrong.
